summary: Iteration 2 focus: validate the Vercel `uv pip install` fix (pyproject.toml + uv.lock + repo-root requirements.txt + .python-version). Ran two local Vercel-equivalent simulations on Python 3.12.13 — both exited 0 with all deps installing cleanly. Backend still healthy (db_reachable:true, 49 routes). Full pytest suite re-run: 48/49 pass (identical to iteration 1; the single failure is the same known cosmetic 500-vs-404 on invalid UUID and is NOT related to this iteration's changes). No regressions introduced by the config changes.

minor.issue: Still returns 500 instead of 404 when book_id is not a valid UUID (Supabase raises APIError before empty-result path). Pre-existing, unrelated to this iteration.

test_1_uv_sync_frozen.command: cd /tmp/vercel_sim && uv sync --frozen (with pyproject.toml + uv.lock + requirements.txt + .python-version)
test_1_uv_sync_frozen.note: 36 external deps installed from lockfile in 397ms; 37th 'package' is the root project itself (bookbridge-india-api virtual source). Total lockfile entries = 37 as expected.

test_2_uv_pip_install_requirements.command: cd /tmp/vercel_sim2 && uv venv --python 3.12 .venv && uv pip install -r requirements.txt (fresh venv, no lockfile)
test_2_uv_pip_install_requirements.note: All 8 top-level pins + transitive deps resolved cleanly on fresh Python 3.12.13 venv. This is the closest local proxy to what Vercel's default `uv pip install` step runs.

vercel_build_simulation.conclusion: Both simulations exit 0. The root cause of the previous `uv pip install exited 1` on Vercel is very likely resolved: pyproject.toml pins Python ~=3.12.0, uv.lock is committed so no re-resolution is needed, python-downloads='never' guards against Vercel's sandbox trying to fetch a Python interpreter, and all 8 deps are explicitly pinned in three redundant places (pyproject/lock, root requirements.txt, api/requirements.txt).

vercel_file_structure_check.uv_lock: OK (53369 bytes, 37 name entries incl. root)
vercel_file_structure_check.vercel_json: OK (runtime pin removed as intended; buildCommand + rewrites intact)
vercel_file_structure_check.vercelignore_check: OK — does NOT exclude pyproject.toml, uv.lock, requirements.txt, or api/
vercel_file_structure_check.pinned_versions_identical: YES — /app/requirements.txt and /app/api/requirements.txt are byte-identical; pyproject.toml pins match (fastapi==0.116.1, pydantic==2.11.0, PyJWT==2.12.1, cryptography==44.0.1, bcrypt==4.1.3, supabase==2.16.0, python-dotenv==1.1.0, email-validator==2.3.0)

backend_health_check.note: Preview pod runs Python 3.11 (unchanged); Vercel will run 3.12 per .python-version. Import test: `from server import app` yields 49 routes — no config change broke local imports.

pytest_regression.delta_vs_iteration_1: 0 new failures, 0 previously-failing now passing — identical outcome

action_items: SAFE TO REDEPLOY on Vercel: push /app/pyproject.toml, /app/uv.lock, /app/requirements.txt, /app/.python-version, /app/api/requirements.txt, /app/vercel.json to GitHub and trigger Vercel redeploy.
(Optional, unrelated) Guard GET /api/books/{id} against non-UUID input to convert 500 → 404 — carried over from iteration 1.
critical_code_review_comments: pyproject.toml uses `requires-python = "~=3.12.0"` which pins to 3.12.x only. This is intentional and matches .python-version. If Vercel ever forces 3.13, uv sync --frozen will fail with a clear message — better than a mystery install failure.

Three requirements sources (pyproject.toml, /requirements.txt, /api/requirements.txt) all agree today. Recommend a small CI check (or a comment in each file) to keep them in sync on future dep bumps — drift here would silently reintroduce the very build failure this fix addresses.
vercel.json no longer pins @vercel/python runtime version — Vercel will now pick the latest, which is what supports uv. Confirmed intentional per the change log.
The .vercelignore correctly excludes tests/, memory/, scripts/, *.log, but keeps all three requirements sources shipped to Vercel — good.
test_credentials: admin@bookbridge.in / Admin@123, aditi@demo.in / demo123 (used by pytest suite)
seed_data_creation: None new. Pytest suite creates TEST_-prefixed transient data via API as before.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vercel `uv pip install` fix has been validated locally on Python 3.12.13 with uv 0.11.28: `uv sync --frozen` and `uv pip install -r requirements.txt` both exit 0 in clean dirs containing only the committed config files. Full pytest suite still 48/49 (only known 500-vs-404 on invalid UUID). Final verification of the actual Vercel build requires the user to push to GitHub and let Vercel redeploy — not reproducible from this pod. No frontend changes were made this iteration.
rca of the issue: Original failure: Vercel switched default Python installer to `uv`, which honors pyproject.toml + uv.lock over requirements.txt. Absence of pyproject.toml + presence of a bare requirements.txt caused uv to re-resolve deps against whatever Python interpreter Vercel booted (likely 3.13), which broke on packages like bcrypt==4.1.3 / cryptography==44.0.1 that were not yet Python-3.13-compatible wheels at the time. Mitigation applied by main agent: (1) pyproject.toml pins Python ~=3.12.0, (2) uv.lock commits an exact resolution so no re-resolution occurs, (3) `[tool.uv] python-downloads = 'never'` prevents uv from silently swapping the interpreter, (4) redundant requirements.txt at repo root + api/requirements.txt guarantee fallback if uv is skipped. Local reproduction of `uv sync --frozen` and `uv pip install -r requirements.txt` on Python 3.12.13 both exit 0 — strong signal the deploy will succeed.
